steps.title: Find the size/consumption signal
steps.content: The passage notes that the pygmy right whale is much smaller than the blue whale, which can consume as much as 45,000 kg of krill per day.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D correctly concludes that the blue whale can eat more krill per day than the pygmy right whale, based on the size difference and the given consumption figure.
The other three either introduce unsupported competition, claim equal consumption, or wrongly suggest the pygmy right whale is less likely to eat krill.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
